title: pleopeltis rivularis
intro: Pleopeltis rivularis, also known as the river polypody or river maidenhair fern, is a species of epiphytic or terrestrial fern native to tropical and subtropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Pleopeltis rivularis include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing, as well as its use in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and ground into a powder, which can be used in teas, infusions, or as a topical application.
